title: Dorry Accepts Luffy’s Rum
description: On pages 13–15, dorry accepts luffy’s rum as mr. 3 and his partners move into position as the giants’ duel and the Straw Hats’ journey become part of a trap.
scene: Dorry Accepts Luffy’s Rum. Mr. 3 and his partners move into position as the giants’ duel and the Straw Hats’ journey become part of a trap. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Dorry the giant's home

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Gegya Gyagya Gyagya!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Dahaha haha!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: This is really good, Mr. Giant!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: gegya Gyagyaga! And your little pirate lunchbox is pretty good too!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Munch Munich

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Though it is a little small!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: You bet it is! I would've kicked your ass if you said it tasted bad!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Gyagya Gyagya! Aren't you a funny little one!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: T-They're getting along so well...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: So why're ya living here alone anyways?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Don't you have a village to live in?

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: I do.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: It's called Elbaf, a village for Warriors.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: It's located somewhere on Grand Line...

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: But my village,

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: has a certain law.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: If a quarrel breaks out in my village and you can't settle it on your own,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Then we beseech our God Elbaf for his judgment.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Elbaf is just and bestows divine protection on those in the right.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: He'll ensure the survival of the righteous.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: I've gotten involved in a quarrel myself,

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: And so this island,

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: has served as the Battleground between me and a certain other giant.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: The one who's in the right shall triumph and survive.

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: But it's been 100 years since!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: We just can't seem to settle our duel! Gegyagya Gya!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You've been fighting for over a 100 years!?

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: It's nothing to be surprised about.

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Our life spans are 3 times your puny ones.

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Gegya Gyagya!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: But even if you do live longer,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: How could you still want to fight even after 100 years!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Is there any reason in continuing to fight!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: You're trying to kill each other, aren't you!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Whoa! It's a Humongous volcanic explosion!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Looks like it's about time...

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on dorry accepts luffy’s rum. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
